volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Eunuch (Section 4.4.668b - Section 4.4.712c)

sections.label: Section 4.4.668b
sections.translation: Mercy , I do entreat you !

sections.label: Section 4.4.668c
sections.translation: Oh , do look at that ! How the villain distorts his face .

sections.label: Section 4.4.670
sections.original: Quid huc tibi reditio est ? vestis quid mutatio ?
sections.translation: What means your coming back hither ? Why this change of dress ? What have you to say ? If I had delayed a moment , Pythias , I shouldn ’ t have found him at home : he had just prepared , in this fashion , for flight .

sections.label: Section 4.4.673
sections.translation: Have you caught the fellow , pray ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.673b
sections.translation: Caught him , why not ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.673c
sections.translation: O well done !

sections.label: Section 4.4.674
sections.translation: Upon my faith that really is capital !

sections.label: Section 4.4.674b
sections.translation: Where is he ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.674c
sections.translation: Do you ask the question ? Don ’ t you see him ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.675
sections.translation: See whom , pray ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.675b
sections.translation: This fellow , to be sure ( pointing ) .

sections.label: Section 4.4.675c
sections.translation: What person is this ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.676
sections.original: Qui ad vos deductus hodie est .
sections.translation: The same that was brought to your house to-day .

sections.label: Section 4.4.676b
sections.translation: Not one of our people has ever beheld this person with her eyes , Phaedria .

sections.label: Section 4.4.678
sections.translation: Not beheld him ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.678b
sections.original: An tu hunc credidisti esse , obsecro ,
sections.translation: Prithee , did you fancy that this was he who was brought to our house ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.679b
sections.translation: Why , I had no other .

sections.label: Section 4.4.679c
sections.translation: O dear !

sections.label: Section 4.4.680
sections.original: Ne comparandus hic quidem ad illum est : ille erat
sections.translation: this one really isn ’ t to be compared with the other . He was of a handsome and genteel appearance .

sections.label: Section 4.4.681b
sections.translation: He seemed so , just then , because he was decked out in party-colored clothes : [*] now he appears ugly , for this reason — because he hasn ’ t got them on .

sections.label: Section 4.4.684
sections.original: Tace , obsecro ; quasi vero paulum intersiet .
sections.translation: Prithee , do hold your tongue ; as though indeed the difference was so trifling .

sections.label: Section 4.4.685
sections.original: Ad nos deductus hodie est adolescentulus ,
sections.translation: A young man was brought to our house to-day , whom , really , Phaedria , you would have liked to look upon . This is a withered , antiquated , lethargic , old fellow , with a speckled complexion . [*]

sections.label: Section 4.4.688b
sections.translation: Hah ! What tale is this ? You ’ ll so befool me that I sha ’ n ’ t know what I bought .

sections.label: Section 4.4.690
sections.translation: ( To DORUS .) How now , sirrah , did I not buy you ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.690b
sections.translation: You did buy me .

sections.label: Section 4.4.690c
sections.translation: Bid him answer me in my turn .

sections.label: Section 4.4.691b
sections.translation: Question him .

sections.label: Section 4.4.691c
sections.translation: Did you come here to-day to our house ? ( DORUS shakes his head .) He says , no . But it was the other one that came , about sixteen years of age ; whom Parmeno brought with him .

sections.label: Section 4.4.693b
sections.translation: Well now , in the first place tell me this , where did you get that dress that you have on ? What , are you silent ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.695
sections.translation: Monster of a fellow , are you not going to speak ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.695b
sections.translation: Chaerea came .

sections.label: Section 4.4.696
sections.translation: What , my brother ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.696b
sections.translation: Yes .

sections.label: Section 4.4.696c
sections.translation: When ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.696d
sections.translation: To-day .

sections.label: Section 4.4.696e
sections.translation: How long since ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.696f
sections.translation: Just now .

sections.label: Section 4.4.697
sections.translation: With whom ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.697b
sections.translation: With Parmeno .

sections.label: Section 4.4.697c
sections.translation: Did you know him before ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.698
sections.original: Non ; nec quis esset umquam audieram dicier .
sections.translation: No .

sections.label: Section 4.4.699
sections.original: Unde igitur fratrem meum esse scibas ?
sections.translation: How did you know he was my brother ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.699b
sections.translation: Parmeno

sections.label: Section 4.4.700
sections.original: Dicebat eum esse : is dedit mihi hanc vestem .
sections.translation: said he was . He gave me these clothes .

sections.label: Section 4.4.700b
sections.translation: I ’ m undone !

sections.label: Section 4.4.701
sections.original: Meam ipse induit : post una ambo abierunt foras .
sections.translation: He himself put on mine ; afterward , they both went out together .

sections.label: Section 4.4.702
sections.original: Iam satis credis sobriam esse me , et nihil mentitam tibi ?
sections.translation: Now are you quite satisfied that I am sober , and that we have told you no falsehood ? Is it now sufficiently evident that the girl has been ravished ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.703b
sections.translation: Avaunt , you beast , do you believe what he says ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.704b
sections.original: Quid isti credam ? res ipsa indicat .
sections.translation: What is there to believe ? The thing speaks for itself .

sections.label: Section 4.4.705
sections.original: Concede istuc paululum : audin ? etiam nunc paululum : sat est .
sections.translation: Step aside a little this way . Do you hear ? ( DORUS steps aside .) A little further still . That will do . Now tell me this once more ; did Chaerea take your clothes off you ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.707
sections.translation: He did .

sections.label: Section 4.4.707b
sections.translation: And did he put them on ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.707c
sections.translation: He did .

sections.label: Section 4.4.707d
sections.original: Et pro te huc deductus est ?
sections.translation: And was he brought here instead of you ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.707e
sections.translation: Yes .

sections.label: Section 4.4.708
sections.original: Iupiter magne , o scelestum atque audacem hominem !
sections.translation: Great Jupiter ! O wicked and audacious fellow !

sections.label: Section 4.4.708b
sections.translation: Woe unto me ! Now at last will you believe that we have been insulted in a disgraceful manner ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.710
sections.original: Mirum ni tu credas quod iste dicit : quid agam nescio .
sections.translation: It is no wonder that you believe what the fellow says . ( Aside .) What I ’ m to do I know not . ( Aside to DORUS .) Hark you , deny it all again . ( Aloud .) Can I not this day extract the truth from you ? Did you really see my brother Chaerea ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.712b
sections.translation: No .

sections.label: Section 4.4.712c
sections.translation: He can ’ t be brought to confess without being punished , I see : follow me this way . At one moment he affirms , at another denies . ( Aside .) Ask pardon of me .
